verdicts.title: Folder 'unused-code/backend' actually audits ai-backend; duplicates ai-backend pass; services/backend unaudited
verdicts.note: docs/unused-code/backend/README.md:1 titles itself 'Unused code and smells — services/ai-backend' and README.md:9 scopes out services/backend/backend-facade; both passes date 2026-05-06 over the same service, so services/backend has no audit.

verdicts.title: use-cases README index desynced from folder; 13/14 links dead; data-flow link untracked
verdicts.note: README lists 01-cold-start-first-message.md etc. while git ls-files docs/use-cases shows 01-new-conversation-simple.md etc.; only 06-mcp-installed-not-authenticated.md matches, and services/ai-backend/docs/architecture/data-flow.md is not tracked.

verdicts.title: decomp corpus half-written; indexes overstate coverage; refactor/ + replacement-analysis.md missing

verdicts.title: Workspace-layout facts drifted across topology + two .cursor mirrors + service-boundaries
verdicts.note: docs/architecture/workspace-topology.md:29-31 lists apps/mac|windows 'planned' and omits apps/desktop; .cursor/rules/workspace-architecture.mdc:14 + service-boundaries.mdc:20 repeat it; docs/architecture/service-boundaries.md:20,40 still mark apps/desktop 'Planned' and gated on Phase S.

verdicts.title: multi-tenant-deployment lists RLS/KMS-vault/SIEM as planned while C5-C9 runbooks document them implemented
verdicts.note: docs/architecture/multi-tenant-deployment.md:22-31 lists RLS ('not enabled in application DDL today'), managed vault, SIEM as 'Planned / design-only' while rls-incident-response.md:8-25 (PR 15 policies + rollout) and siem-export.md:1-20 (running pump) describe them as implemented.

verdicts.title: Two ai-backend passes contradict on share_service._collect_sources(sources_visible)
verdicts.note: ai-backend/06-agent-runtime-api-and-services.md:20 marks it High-confidence 'Remove redundant parameter' while backend/cluster-agent-runtime-domain-services.md:30 rules it a 'False positive … No remediation needed' citing call-site reads ~lines 459/468.

verdicts.title: Surviving prior dead-code claims to re-verify at HEAD (curated salvage list)
verdicts.note: All cited docs do carry these claims, but the 'surviving/high-confidence' framing is stale for >=2 enumerated items: at HEAD InboxEventEnvelopeSchema IS imported/used (sse/inbox_adapter.py:17,72) and ToolBudgetMiddleware.check_admit IS wired (runtime_worker/handlers/run.py:1429, capability_tool_wiring.py:64); register_health_routes(app) still passes no checkers (app.py:1317) so readyz-always-ready holds — exactly the re-verification the finding asks for.
